title: precedence table (grammar-pinned)
manager: One authoritative operator-precedence table, pinned in the grammar, so `a + b * c` and every other mixed expression parse the same way forever and on every tool that reads FSL. Precedence that drifts between a parser and a formatter is a silent meaning-change — the worst kind, because the source looks unchanged — so nailing it in the grammar and fuzzing it with the dragon makes the parse a fact rather than a per-implementation opinion.
cs: full precedence/associativity table in the pinned grammar (C3); dragon-fuzzed; formatter and the second implementation conform to it; covers arithmetic/comparison/boolean/bitwise/pipe/concat.
